{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27348","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27348/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27348/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27348/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27348","id":2507487453,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86VdTjd","number":27348,"title":"BUG: NumPy types don't return NotImplemented for operator overloading of operands they can't handle","user":{"login":"Mou141","id":17607121,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE3NjA3MTIx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/17607121?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mou141","html_url":"https://github.com/Mou141","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mou141/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mou141/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mou141/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mou141/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mou141/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mou141/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mou141/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mou141/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Mou141/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359214,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5MjE0","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/00%20-%20Bug","name":"00 - Bug","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-05T11:12:31Z","updated_at":"2024-09-05T11:12:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

I have been using hist version 2.7.2 with NumPy [https://pypi.org/project/hist/](https://pypi.org/project/hist/). Hist's histogram objects overload the multiply operator to allow histograms to be scaled by scalar values. The `__mul__` method on hist.Hist can handle both python numeric types and NumPy's numeric types.

Python evaluates operator overloading from left to right. The `__mul__` method on the left operand is evaluated first. If that method returns `NotImplemented`, the `__mul__` method on the right operand is evaluated. Internally, `hist.Hist` stores its bin counts and variances as a structured NumPy array.

When a python numeric type is multiplied by a `hist.Hist` object (e.g. `4.0 * h`), the `__mul__` method on the python object returns `NotImplemented` and `__mul__` is called on the `hist.Hist` object.

When a NumPy numeric type is multiplied by a `hist.Hist` object (e.g. `np.float64(12.0) * h`), the `__mul__` method on the NumPy type passes the object to `np.multiply` which cannot multiply the `hist.Hist` object. `numpy.core._exceptions._UFuncNoLoopError` is raised.

Because `NotImplemented` is not returned, python doesn't delegate to the `__mul__` method of the `hist.Hist` object.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy as np
import hist

h = hist.Hist.new.Regular(5, 0.0, 1.0, flow=True).Weight()
h.fill(np.random.random(100))

np.float64(12.0) * h
```


### Error message:

```shell
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
numpy.core._exceptions._UFuncNoLoopError: ufunc 'multiply' did not contain a loop with signature matching types (dtype('float64'), dtype([('value', '<f8'), ('variance', '<f8')])) -> None
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

1.26.4
3.10.12 (main, Jul 29 2024, 16:56:48) [GCC 11.4.0]

### Context for the issue:

Code which uses NumPy often mixes python `float` and NumPy floating point types. It is often not known at runtime which will be used. Having `4.0 * obj` evaluating differently from `np.float64(4.0) * obj` (where `obj` is an arbitrary object implementing `__mul__`) leads to intermittent errors that are very difficult to debug.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27348/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27348/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
